evals.prompt: Set up LiteLLM with OpenRouter so Codex can use it through a custom model provider. Show where config belongs and how to verify it.
evals.expected_output: Routes to llm-gateway, separates repo skill guidance from user-level Codex config, uses env vars for keys, validates LiteLLM/OpenRouter and /v1/responses compatibility before Codex routing.

evals.prompt: Review this LiteLLM proxy config before we share it with the team. Check OpenRouter key handling, virtual keys, logs, budgets, and fallbacks.
evals.expected_output: Focuses on gateway security, secret placement, scoped keys, logging/redaction, provider/model allowlists, budgets, rate limits, fallback privacy, and rollback.

evals.prompt: Fix this failing unit test in the date parser and keep the diff small.
evals.expected_output: Should not require llm-gateway; senior-developer or ponytail is a better fit unless model proxy setup is central.
